reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We must write a plan, then call review_plan. According to workflow: first respond with plan. Then call review_plan. After approval, we must modify /etc/security/pwquality.conf (or .conf.d) to set lcredit to -1? Actually need to ensure at least one lowercase character. The lcredit parameter: negative number -> requires that many lowercase characters. So -1 means at least one lowercase. Actually the docs: -1 means require one lowercase character. So we should set lcredit=-1.

Check whether existing /etc/security/pwquality.conf or .conf files contain lcredit. We need to read file(s). On Rocky Linux, pwquality config usually /etc/security/pwquality.conf. So read that file. If lcredit line exists, modify. If not, append. Use sed -i 's/^#?lcredit\s*=.*$/lcredit = -1/' to modify. If not found, append 'lcredit = -1' to file.

We must do it with minimal steps.

So plan: Read /etc/security/pwquality.conf. Use sed to modify or append. Then optionally check /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/*.conf too, but rule likely expects main file. Typically it's fine to modify main.

So produce plan description. Then call review_plan.
